<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> news. Horror after horror from not just down the street but from across the<|7.66|> <|7.66|> globe. We were designed to absorb news of our family, of our neighbor, of our<|17.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> village.<|0.58|> <|2.08|> And we, when we are bombarded with the tragedies of the whole world, we can feel so overwhelmed<|10.94|> <|10.94|> that we too simply blow a fuse and shut down.<|16.36|> <|18.22|> Psychic numbing.<|19.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|4.08|> Friends, I know that the term thoughts and prayers gets a bad rap.<|10.34|> <|12.22|> I know it has become the suspect and often insincere sounding go-to<|19.56|> <|20.52|> for politicians and other public figures.<|23.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I can't speak for anyone else, but I believe as Christians, I actually believe that it's thoughts and prayers that save us from psychic numbing.<|17.36|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If data and statistics shut off the emotional parts of our brain, let us fill our thoughts with the names and images and stories of these children.<|17.70|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> children. Let us remember in our thoughts these families who are grieving.
